title: Prologue | Do We Get to Win This Time?
format: Sean and Amanda hand the feed to Brian Raftery's new narrative series on how Hollywood turned the Vietnam War into its own genre.

segments.heading: How Hollywood made the Vietnam War
segments.note: feed takeover: prologue for Raftery's eight-part narrative series

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: Robert Duvall's beach assault opens the episode; Coppola's Cannes boast that the film 'is Vietnam' gets fact-checked on the spot.

films.title: Full Metal Jacket
films.note: R. Lee Ermey chewing out Marines is the other defining image Raftery reaches for.

films.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
films.note: Stallone going back to 'win' the war he lost the first time.

films.title: The Losers
films.note: The drive-in end of the genre: a Vietnam-vet biker caper Raftery quotes the trailer copy for.

films.title: The Deer Hunter
films.note: Cited for Michael Cimino's famously grueling shoot.

films.title: Platoon
films.note: Oliver Stone sent his young cast into the jungle for weeks to prep.

films.title: Born on the Fourth of July
films.note: One of the films Marine vet and military advisor Dale Dye worked on.

films.title: Good Morning, Vietnam
films.note: The R-rated movie an 11-year-old Raftery talked his parents into.

films.title: Heaven & Earth
films.note: Oliver Stone's adaptation of Le Ly Hayslip's memoir; Hayslip herself appears to question what the film gets wrong.

Comics: Peter Porker and the Spectacular Spider-Ham

Born on the Fourth of July.title: Born on the Fourth of July
